import re
import os
import datetime
from collections import Counter

class LogEntry:
    def __init__(self, timestamp_str, level, component, message):
        self.timestamp = datetime.datetime.strptime(timestamp_str, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
        self.level = level
        self.component = component
        self.message = message

def parse_log_file(filepath):
    log_pattern = re.compile(r"^\[(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2})\] \[([A-Z]+)\] \[([^\]]+)\] (.*)$")
    
    with open(filepath, 'r', encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore') as f:
        for line in f:
            match = log_pattern.match(line.strip())
            if match:
                try:
                    timestamp_str, level, component, message = match.groups()
                    yield LogEntry(timestamp_str, level, component, message)
                except ValueError:
                    pass

def analyze_logs(log_entry_generator):
    total_entries = 0
    level_counts = Counter()
    events_per_hour = Counter()
    component_counts = Counter()
    error_messages = Counter()
    
    for entry in log_entry_generator:
        total_entries += 1
        level_counts[entry.level] += 1
        
        hour_key = entry.timestamp.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H")
        events_per_hour[hour_key] += 1
        
        component_counts[entry.component] += 1
        
        if entry.level == "ERROR":
            error_messages[entry.message] += 1
            
    return {
        "total_entries": total_entries,
        "level_distribution": level_counts,
        "events_per_hour": events_per_hour,
        "component_distribution": component_counts,
        "top_error_messages": error_messages.most_common(5)
    }

def print_statistics(stats):
    print("--- Log Analysis Statistics ---")
    print(f"Total Log Entries: {stats['total_entries']}")
    print("\nLog Level Distribution:")
    for level, count in stats['level_distribution'].most_common():
        print(f"  {level}: {count}")

    print("\nEvents Per Hour (Top 10):")
    for hour, count in stats['events_per_hour'].most_common(10):
        print(f"  {hour}: {count}")

    print("\nComponent Distribution (Top 10):")
    for component, count in stats['component_distribution'].most_common(10):
        print(f"  {component}: {count}")

    print("\nTop 5 Error Messages:")
    if stats['top_error_messages']:
        for message, count in stats['top_error_messages']:
            print(f"  [{count} times] {message}")
    else:
        print("  No ERROR level entries found.")

def main():
    log_file_path = "sample.log"

    if not os.path.exists(log_file_path):
        with open(log_file_path, "w") as f:
            f.write("[2023-10-27 10:00:01] [INFO] [System] Application started.\n")
            f.write("[2023-10-27 10:00:05] [DEBUG] [Auth] User 'admin' attempting login.\n")
            f.write("[2023-10-27 10:00:10] [ERROR] [Database] Failed to connect to DB.\n")
            f.write("[2023-10-27 10:00:15] [INFO] [WebServer] Request GET /dashboard from 192.168.1.100\n")
            f.write("[2023-10-27 10:00:20] [WARN] [Auth] Invalid password for user 'admin'.\n")
            f.write("[2023-10-27 10:00:25] [INFO] [System] Health check passed.\n")
            f.write("[2023-10-27 10:00:30] [ERROR] [WebServer] 500 Internal Server Error for /api/data.\n")
            f.write("[2023-10-27 10:00:35] [INFO] [Auth] User 'guest' logged in.\n")
            f.write("[2023-10-27 11:00:01] [INFO] [System] Background task started.\n")
            f.write("[2023-10-27 11:00:05] [DEBUG] [Processor] Processing item 123.\n")
            f.write("[2023-10-27 11:00:10] [ERROR] [Database] Connection pool exhausted.\n")
            f.write("[2022-10-27 10:00:01] [INFO] [System] Old log entry from a different year.\n")
            f.write("[2023-10-27 10:00:10] [ERROR] [Database] Failed to connect to DB.\n")
            f.write("This is an unparsable line.\n")
            f.write("[2023-10-27 10:00:10] [CUSTOM] [ServiceA] Custom level message.\n")
            f.write("[2023-10-27 10:00:10] [ERROR] [Database] Failed to connect to DB. (Another instance)\n")

    print(f"Analyzing log file: {log_file_path}")
    
    log_entry_generator = parse_log_file(log_file_path)
    statistics = analyze_logs(log_entry_generator)
    
    if statistics["total_entries"] == 0:
        print("No parsable log entries found.")
        return

    print_statistics(statistics)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
